downstream_consumers: headerStatus propagated to all elements of each file

risks.title: Filtering 'default' may hide genuine header mismatches
risks.mitigation: The fix is directional: 'default' is ONLY filtered from astSet, not from headerSet. If a header mistakenly declares 'default', that would still surface as headerSet-has but astSet-lacks mismatch. Only the reverse (astSet has 'default', header doesn't) is suppressed.

risks.title: Re-exports ('export { X as default }') may also emit 'default'
risks.mitigation: Same fix applies — 'default' as a re-export alias is also not declared in @exports headers. Filtering is correct for all forms.

decision_records.decision: Filter 'default' from astSet only (not from headerSet)
decision_records.rationale: Preserves ability to detect genuine mismatches where a header incorrectly claims 'default'. Only suppresses the one-directional false positive where AST has 'default' but header doesn't.

decision_records.decision: Do not modify relationship-extractor.ts to stop emitting 'default'
decision_records.rationale: The extractor may be used by other consumers that legitimately want to know about default exports. The fix belongs at the comparison boundary, not the extraction layer.
